predictions.title: World models will have their “ChatGPT moment”
predictions.description: Mainstream breakthrough for world models beyond synthetic demos.

updates.content: LoViF 2026 The First Challenge on Holistic Quality Assessment for 4D World Model (PhyScore) (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05187) - first large-scale competition on holistic quality assessment of world-model-generated videos across both 2D and 4D generation settings, addressing the gap that perceptual quality alone is insufficient for world model evaluation. Also: Driver-WM: A Driver-Centric Traffic-Conditioned Latent World Model for In-Cabin Dynamics Rollout (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05092) - driver-centric latent world model forecasting human-in-the-loop behavior during shared-control transitions for L2/L3 driving automation. Also: ReflectDrive-2: Reinforcement-Learning-Aligned Self-Editing for Discrete Diffusion Driving (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04647) - masked discrete diffusion planner with RL-aligned self-editing for autonomous driving world models.
updates.reasoning: New evaluation infrastructure for world model video quality assessment (PhyScore challenge), driver-centric world model for human-in-the-loop autonomy, and RL-aligned discrete diffusion world model for driving -- advancing world model breadth and evaluation rigor.

updates.content: A Benchmark for Interactive World Models with a Unified Action Generation Framework (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03941) - first large-scale benchmark and unified action generation framework for evaluating interactive world models. Also: RoboAlign-R1: Distilled Multimodal Reward Alignment for Robot Video World Models (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03821) - aligns robot video world models with instruction following and physical plausibility via multimodal reward distillation.
updates.reasoning: New benchmark infrastructure for interactive world models and alignment techniques for robot video world models, both addressing key gaps on the path to a ChatGPT-level breakthrough for world models.

updates.content: Shadow-Loom: Causal Reasoning over Graphical World Model of Narratives (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02475) - turns narratives into versioned graphical world models with causal reasoning. Also: Embody4D: A Generalist 4D World Model for Embodied AI (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01799) - generalist 4D world model for embodied AI across multi-view 3D representation.
updates.reasoning: New causal world model framework for narratives and a generalist 4D world model for embodied AI, advancing toward a ChatGPT-level breakthrough for world models.

updates.content: Physically Native World Models: A Hamiltonian Perspective on Generative World Modeling (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00412) - Hamiltonian-constrained world models unifying 2D video gen, 3D reconstruction, and JEPA latent dynamics. Also: World Model for Robot Learning: A Comprehensive Survey (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00080) - survey of world models transforming robot learning.
updates.reasoning: New theoretical paradigm for physically-native world models and a comprehensive survey, both advancing toward a ChatGPT-level breakthrough.

updates.content: World2Minecraft: Occupancy-Driven Simulated Scenes Construction (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27578v1) - Constructs simulation environments from real-world occupancy data, advancing world model infrastructure.
updates.reasoning: World model simulation infrastructure advances, bringing embodied world models closer to practical deployment.

updates.content: Visual Generation in the New Era: An Evolution from Atomic Mapping to Agentic World Modeling (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28185v1) - Traces the evolution of visual generation toward agentic world models that combine spatial reasoning, persistent state, and interactive editing.
updates.reasoning: Progress toward a ChatGPT moment for world models, evolving from pure generation to agentic world modeling.

updates.content: HERMES++: A Unified Driving World Model for 3D Scene Understanding and Generation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28196v1) - integrates 3D scene understanding and future geometry prediction in a single world model framework.
updates.reasoning: Advances in unified world models for autonomous driving with LLM-enhanced reasoning.

updates.content: Outer-Crust Equations of State for Neutron Stars (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26952v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: How Fast Should a Model Commit to Supervision? (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25907v1) discusses training reasoning models.
updates.reasoning: New research relevant to world models.

updates.content: HaM-World: Soft-Hamiltonian World Models with Selective Memory for Planning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05951) - Hamiltonian-constrained world model with selective memory for long-horizon planning stability. Also: Executable World Models for ARC-AGI-3 in the Era of Coding Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05138) - coding agent system with executable Python world model for ARC-AGI-3 reasoning. Also: PhysForge: Generating Physics-Grounded 3D Assets for Interactive Virtual World (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05163) - synthesizes physics-grounded 3D assets for interactive virtual worlds and embodied AI.
updates.reasoning: HaM-World introduces Hamiltonian physical structure with selective memory for planner-facing latents, advancing world model planning stability. Executable world models for ARC coding agents bring world models to interactive reasoning benchmarks. PhysForge bridges world model physics to 3D asset generation for embodied AI.

predictions.title: Non-language models will get more love
predictions.description: Momentum around action models and VLAs (Vision-Language-Action).

updates.content: ConsisVLA-4D: Advancing Spatiotemporal Consistency in Efficient 3D-Perception and 4D-Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05126) - VLA model addressing spatiotemporal perception and reasoning limitations by integrating 3D perception with 4D temporal reasoning. Also: From Pixels to Tokens: A Systematic Study of Latent Action Supervision for Vision-Language-Action Models (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04678) - structures the study of latent action supervision across heterogeneous datasets for consistent VLA modeling. Also: When Life Gives You BC, Make Q-functions: Extracting Q-values from Behavior Cloning for On-Robot Reinforcement Learning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05172) - bridges behavior cloning to online RL without reward engineering.
updates.reasoning: VLA momentum continues with ConsisVLA-4D adding 3D-aware spatiotemporal reasoning to VLAs, latent action supervision systematizing heterogeneous VLA training, and a new bridge from behavior cloning to online RL for action models.

updates.content: RLDX-1 Technical Report (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03269) - addresses VLA generalization challenges through task decomposition, enabling generalist robotic policies to handle diverse manipulation tasks with improved robustness.
updates.reasoning: New VLA approach tackling the key challenge of task diversity and generalization in generalist robotic policies, advancing the non-language action model paradigm.

updates.content: Seeing Realism from Simulation: Efficient Video Transfer for VLA Data Augmentation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02757) - sim-to-real VLA data augmentation. Also: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02881) - action reasoning VLA for real-world robots. Also: Latent Bridge: Feature Delta Prediction for Efficient Dual-System VLA Inference (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02739). Also: VILAS: Low-cost VLA Platform with Soft Grasping (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02037).
updates.reasoning: VLA momentum accelerating — MolmoAct2 for real-world deployment, sim-to-real transfer for data augmentation, efficient dual-system inference, and low-cost hardware for VLA policy learning.

updates.content: Thinking in Text and Images: Interleaved Vision--Language Reasoning Traces for Long-Horizon Robot Manipulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00438) - VLA policy with interleaved text-image reasoning combining spatial constraints and causal order for long-horizon manipulation.
updates.reasoning: VLA model advances with multimodal reasoning traces, furthering the non-language action model paradigm.

updates.content: A Pattern Language for Resilient Visual Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28001v1) - Software architecture patterns for deploying multimodal foundation models (including VLAs) in enterprise ecosystems.
updates.reasoning: Architecture patterns enabling resilient VLA-based visual agents for production deployment.

updates.content: LaST-R1: Reinforcing Action via Adaptive Physical Latent Reasoning for VLA Models (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28192v1) - Applies RL directly to VLA models for robotic manipulation using adaptive physical latent reasoning. Also: FreeOcc: Training-Free Embodied Open-Vocabulary Occupancy Prediction (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28115v1).
updates.reasoning: Direct advances in vision-language-action models with RL-based reasoning and training-free embodied scene understanding.

updates.content: MotuBrain: An Advanced World Action Model for Robot Control (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27792v1) - Vision-Language-Action (VLA) model combining video generation with world action modeling for robotics.
updates.reasoning: Direct progress on VLA/world action models combining vision, language, and action.

updates.content: Outer-Crust Equations of State for Neutron Stars (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26952v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25914v1) explores VLA-related benchmarking.
updates.reasoning: Advancements in agentic benchmarking.

updates.content: VLA-GSE: Boosting Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning in VLA with Generalized and Specialized Experts (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06175) - new PEFT approach for VLA models using generalized and specialized expert routing to prevent overfitting and catastrophic forgetting. Also: DexSim2Real: Foundation Model-Guided Sim-to-Real Transfer for Generalizable Dexterous Manipulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05241) - sim-to-real transfer for dexterous manipulation using foundation models. Also: Creative Robot Tool Use by Counterfactual Reasoning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05411) - robots discover novel tool use through counterfactual reasoning.
updates.reasoning: VLA momentum continues with architectural innovations for parameter-efficient fine-tuning preventing catastrophic forgetting, sim-to-real transfer enabling practical dexterous manipulation deployment, and counterfactual reasoning expanding VLA capabilities to creative physical tool use.

predictions.title: Learning will become distributed
predictions.description: Agents continuously learning by observing and copying other agents.

updates.content: Graph-SND: Sparse Aggregation for Behavioral Diversity in Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05020) - replaces quadratic pairwise distance computation with weighted sparse aggregation for behavioral diversity in multi-agent RL, enabling scalable distributed agent teams. Also: SensingAgents: A Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework for Robust IMU Activity Recognition (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04608) - multi-agent collaborative framework for sensor-based activity recognition. Also: Self-Induced Outcome Potential: Turn-Level Credit Assignment for Agents without Verifiers (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04984) - enables distributed credit assignment in long-horizon LLM agents without process-level human annotation.
updates.reasoning: Distributed learning advances through scalable multi-agent RL diversity metrics, collaborative sensor agent frameworks, and turn-level credit assignment that removes the need for human-annotated process rewards -- all enabling agents to learn from distributed interactions.

updates.content: Mechanical Conscience: A Mathematical Framework for Dependability of Machine Intelligence (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03847) - formal framework for dependability in distributed collaborative intelligence (DCI) systems. Also: Hierarchical Federated Learning for Networked AI: From Communication Saving to Architecture-Aware Design (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00931) - rethinks hierarchy in federated learning as a fundamental distributed optimization structure.
updates.reasoning: Advances in distributed collaborative intelligence through formal dependability frameworks and architecture-aware federated learning, strengthening the distributed learning paradigm.

updates.content: When Stress Becomes Signal: Detecting Antifragility-Compatible Regimes in Multi-Agent LLM Systems (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02463) - multi-agent LLM systems studied for stress-tolerance. Also: Planner Matters! An Efficient Multi-agent Collaboration Framework for Long-horizon Planning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02168).
updates.reasoning: Distributed learning through multi-agent LLM systems: antifragility analysis of multi-agent collaboration and efficient planning frameworks for long-horizon tasks.

updates.content: FedHarmony: Harmonizing Heterogeneous Label Correlations in Federated Multi-Label Learning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28024) - federated multi-label learning harmonizing distributed label correlations. Also: AdaBFL: Multi-Layer Defensive Adaptive Aggregation for Byzantine-Robust Federated Learning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27434) - robust distributed learning.
updates.reasoning: Advances in distributed/federated learning across heterogeneous data sources, supporting decentralized agent learning.

updates.content: GUI Agents with Reinforcement Learning: Toward Digital Inhabitants (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27955v1) - Applies RL to GUI agents so they continuously learn digital environments. Also: AutoSurfer -- Teaching Web Agents through Comprehensive Surfing, Learning, and Modeling (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27253v1) - web agents learning autonomously by surfing and modeling.
updates.reasoning: Distributed learning through agent-environment interaction: GUI agents learning via RL and web agents learning autonomously.

updates.content: CastFlow: Learning Role-Specialized Agentic Workflows for Time Series Forecasting (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27840v1) - Distributed multi-agent learning framework where agents specialize into roles and collaborate.
updates.reasoning: Embodies distributed learning through agent role specialization and collaboration.

updates.content: Safe Bilevel Delegation (SBD): A Formal Framework for Runtime Delegation Safety in Multi-Agent Systems (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27358v1) - formal framework for safe subtask delegation between LLM agents.
updates.reasoning: Advances in distributed multi-agent learning with formal delegation safety guarantees.

updates.content: Outer-Crust Equations of State for Neutron Stars (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26952v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25917v1) explores distributed agent learning.
updates.reasoning: Direct research on multi-agent learning.

updates.content: SPARK: Self-Play with Asymmetric Reward from Knowledge Graphs (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05546) - self-play framework for distributed agent learning using asymmetric reward signals from knowledge graphs. Also: Active Learning for Communication Structure Optimization in LLM-Based Multi-Agent Systems (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05703) - optimizes communication topology in distributed LLM agent teams via active learning. Also: Improving the Efficiency of Language Agent Teams with Adaptive Task Graphs (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06320) - adaptive task graph construction balancing structured coordination with unstructured exploration in agent teams.
updates.reasoning: Distributed agent team learning advances: self-play with knowledge graph rewards for scalable training, active learning for optimizing communication structures in multi-agent LLM systems, and adaptive task graphs enabling efficient coordination in language agent teams.

predictions.title: AI, control theory, and formal methods will converge
predictions.description: Intersection for verification, stability, and reliability of RL systems.

updates.content: A Closed-Form Dual-Barrier CBF Safety Filter for Holonomic Robots on Incrementally Built Occupancy Grid Maps (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05182) - control barrier function (CBF) safety filter for real-time safety-critical robot control in unknown environments, merging control theory with RL-based planning. Also: InterFuserDVS: Event-Enhanced Sensor Fusion for Safe RL-Based Decision Making (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04355) - event-enhanced sensor fusion using Dynamic Vision Sensors for safe RL in autonomous driving. Also: AgentTrust: Runtime Safety Evaluation and Interception for AI Agent Tool Use (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04785) - formal runtime safety evaluation for AI agent tool calls.
updates.reasoning: Direct convergence of control theory (CBF safety filters) and RL for safe autonomous systems, plus formal runtime safety verification for AI agents -- strong evidence for the AI-control-formal methods convergence prediction.

updates.content: Lyapunov-Guided Self-Alignment: Test-Time Adaptation for Offline Safe Reinforcement Learning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26516) - uses Lyapunov functions (control theory) to guide safe self-alignment in offline RL at test time. Also: Model-Based Proactive Cost Generation for Learning Safe Policies Offline with Limited Violation Data (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01356) - model-based safety cost generation for offline RL in safety-critical domains.
updates.reasoning: Direct convergence of control theory (Lyapunov functions) with RL for safe policy adaptation and model-based safety guarantees, advancing the intersection of formal methods and reinforcement learning.

updates.content: Enhancing RL Generalizability in Robotics through SHAP Analysis of Algorithms and Hyperparameters (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02867) - RL generalization analysis via SHAP for robotics control. Also: A Relative Ignorability Framework for Decision-Relevant Observability in Control Theory and RL (http://arxiv.org/abs/2504.07722v6).
updates.reasoning: Convergence of RL and control theory through interpretability analysis of RL for robotics and formal frameworks bridging control theory observability with RL decision-making.

updates.content: SAVGO: Learning State-Action Value Geometry with Cosine Similarity for Continuous Control (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00787) - geometry-aware RL bridging representation learning and control. Also: Approximations and Learning for Decentralized Stochastic Control (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00160) - connects control theory and RL for decentralized control.
updates.reasoning: Convergence of control theory (stochastic control) and RL (value-based learning) for verifiable continuous control.

updates.content: Uncertainty-Aware Predictive Safety Filters for Probabilistic Neural Network Dynamics (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26836v1) - Combines model predictive control with probabilistic neural networks for safe RL exploration. Also: PALCAS: A Priority-Aware Lane Change Advisory System using Federated RL (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27118v1) - Federated RL with control-theoretic safety for autonomous vehicles.
updates.reasoning: Direct convergence of control theory (predictive safety filters, MPC) and RL (probabilistic NNs, federated RL) for safe autonomous systems.

updates.content: Computing Equilibrium beyond Unilateral Deviation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28186v1) extends game-theoretic equilibrium concepts for multi-agent systems. Also: Exploration Hacking: Can LLMs Learn to Resist RL Training? (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28182v1) reveals critical failure modes in RL training.
updates.reasoning: Intersection of game theory, RL, and verification — both advancing formal guarantees for AI systems.

updates.content: Global Optimality for Constrained Exploration via Penalty Regularization (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28144v1) - connects control theory and RL for constrained maximum-entropy exploration.
updates.reasoning: Intersection of control theory (constrained optimization), RL, and formal methods for exploration.

updates.content: Outer-Crust Equations of State for Neutron Stars (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26952v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: How Fast Should a Model Commit to Supervision? (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25907v1) - training stability and supervision.
updates.reasoning: Supervision dynamics in RL training.

updates.content: Safety Certification is Classification (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06087) - reframes safety certification of dynamical systems under uncertainty as a classification problem, directly merging formal methods with ML-based safety verification. Also: Long-Horizon Q-Learning: Accurate Value Learning via n-Step Inequalities (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05812) - theoretical advances connecting long-horizon RL value learning with control-theoretic n-step inequalities for improved convergence.
updates.reasoning: Direct convergence evidence: Safety Certification is Classification bridges formal safety certification and ML classification for dynamical systems under uncertainty. Long-Horizon Q-Learning connects RL value learning with n-step control theory inequalities.

predictions.title: Prompt and context engineering will be abstracted away
predictions.description: Handled by prompt compilers and optimizers (desired-state programming).

updates.content: LCM: Lossless Context Management (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04050) - deterministic architecture for LLM memory using recursive context compression and task partitioning, outperforming Claude Code on long-context tasks while abstracting away all manual context/prompt management. Also: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05191) - adaptive context management that automatically orchestrates growing working context. Also: Uno-Orchestra: Parsimonious Agent Routing via Selective Delegation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05007) - jointly optimizes decomposition depth, worker choice, and inference budget, abstracting away manual agent orchestration.
updates.reasoning: Lossless Context Management provides automatic lossless context compression outperforming frontier coding agents, elastic context orchestration automates search agent memory management, and parsimonious routing eliminates hand-engineered agent orchestration.

updates.content: Reinforced Agent: Inference-Time Feedback for Tool-Calling Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27233) - replaces prompt tuning with inference-time feedback, eliminating manual prompt engineering for tool-calling agents. Also: Learning Evidence Highlighting for Frozen LLMs (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.22565) - automatically optimizes how context is presented to frozen LLMs, abstracting away evidence selection from reasoning.
updates.reasoning: Two advances in abstracting prompt engineering: inference-time feedback replaces manual prompt tuning entirely, and automatic context optimization separates evidence selection from reasoning.

updates.content: Exploiting LLM-as-a-Judge Disposition on Free Text Legal QA via Prompt Optimization (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.20726v2) - automatic task prompt optimization. Also: ContraPrompt: Contrastive Prompt Optimization via Dyadic Reasoning Trace Analysis (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17937). Also: Prompt Optimization Enables Stable Algorithmic Collusion in LLM Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17774).
updates.reasoning: Prompt optimization continues to mature as an automated discipline, with contrastive optimization, legal-domain automation, and market stability analysis — pushing prompt engineering further behind the scenes.

updates.content: Agent Capsules: Quality-Gated Granularity Control for Multi-Agent LLM Pipelines (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00410) - adaptive runtime treating multi-agent pipeline execution as optimization, abstracting granularity decisions from developers.
updates.reasoning: Auto-mates agent orchestration granularity, further abstracting prompt/context engineering behind runtime optimization.

updates.content: Co-Director: Agentic Generative Video Storytelling (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24842v1) - Automated agentic pipeline for video storytelling that abstracts away prompt engineering into high-level creative direction.
updates.reasoning: Agentic pipeline abstraction that hides prompt-level complexity behind higher-level creative intent specification.

updates.content: In-Context Prompting Obsoletes Agent Orchestration for Procedural Tasks (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27891v1) - Directly argues that in-context prompting replaces the need for explicit agent orchestration frameworks.
updates.reasoning: Strong evidence for prompt engineering being abstracted away — in-context prompting subsumes orchestration entirely.

updates.content: OMEGA: Optimizing Machine Learning by Evaluating Generated Algorithms (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26211v1) - end-to-end framework combining structured meta-prompting with automated code generation.
updates.reasoning: Automated prompt engineering pipeline that abstracts away manual prompt tuning.

updates.content: Optimizing Dynamic Metasurface Antenna Configurations for Direction-of-Arrival and Polarization Estimation Using an Experimentally Calibrated Multiport-Network Model (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26948v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: How Fast Should a Model Commit to Supervision? (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25907v1) - implications for training automation.
updates.reasoning: Research related to training efficiency.

updates.content: Nonsense Helps: Prompt Space Perturbation Broadens Reasoning Exploration (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05566) - automated prompt perturbation for expanding reasoning search space without manual prompt engineering. Also: Uno-Orchestra: Parsimonious Agent Routing via Selective Delegation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05007) - jointly optimizes decomposition depth, worker choice, and inference budget, abstracting away manual agent orchestration. Also: TACT: Mitigating Overthinking and Overacting in Coding Agents via Activation Steering (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05980) - automated activation steering reduces reasoning overhead without prompt tuning.
updates.reasoning: Prompt engineering continues to be abstracted away: automated prompt space perturbation for broader exploration, joint optimization of routing and budgets eliminating manual orchestration, and activation steering removing the need for prompt-based behavior tuning.

predictions.title: New distribution mechanisms for simple apps will emerge
predictions.description: Shipping vibes instead of code; on-demand customization.

updates.content: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05170) - LLM agent autonomously designs and builds a hardware inference accelerator, demonstrating agent-as-distribution for complex hardware design. Also: OpenSearch-VL: An Open Recipe for Frontier Multimodal Search Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05185) - open recipe for reproducible multimodal search agents, enabling distribution of agent capabilities as recipes. Also: DAO-Enabled Decentralized Physical AI: A New Paradigm for Human-Machine Collaboration (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04522) - decentralized autonomous organization framework for coordinating humans and autonomous machines in physical-digital systems.
updates.reasoning: New distribution mechanisms: agent-designed hardware accelerators replace traditional software distribution, open recipes enable agent capability distribution, and DAO-based decentralized physical AI creates a new paradigm for coordinating autonomous systems at scale.

updates.content: Scalable Inference Architectures for Compound AI Systems: A Production Deployment Study (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25724) - production deployment study of compound AI systems that compose models, retrievers, and tools, enabling on-demand AI application delivery. Also: Think Before You Act -- A Neurocognitive Governance Model for Autonomous AI Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25684) - governance model enabling safe real-world deployment of autonomous agents.
updates.reasoning: New distribution infrastructure for compound AI systems in production and governance frameworks enabling safe agent deployment, advancing the paradigm of shipping AI capabilities instead of traditional software.

updates.content: Building Persona-Based Agents On Demand: Tailoring Multi-Agent Workflows to User Needs (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27882) - on-demand persona-based multi-agent deployment. Also: A Compound AI Agent for Conversational Grant Discovery (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02366) - domain-specific agent distribution. Also: LiveFMBench: Unveiling Agentic Workflows in Specification Generation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01394).
updates.reasoning: New distribution mechanisms for agentic AI: on-demand persona-based agent workflows, conversational grant discovery agents, and agentic specification generation — shipping capabilities instead of code.

updates.content: Position: agentic AI orchestration should be Bayes-consistent (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00742) - Bayesian decision-making for agent orchestration. Also: Can Coding Agents Reproduce Findings in Computational Materials Science? (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00803) - autonomous agent deployment in scientific research.
updates.reasoning: New distribution mechanisms via Bayesian agent orchestration and autonomous scientific agent deployment.

updates.content: Addressing the Reality Gap: A Three-Tension Framework for Agentic AI Adoption (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27245v1) - Framework for deploying autonomous agentic AI systems in real-world contexts. Also: Scalable Inference Architectures for Compound AI Systems (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25724v1) - Production deployment patterns for compound AI applications.
updates.reasoning: New deployment and distribution architectures enabling on-demand agentic AI in real-world applications.

updates.content: Synthetic Computers at Scale for Long-Horizon Productivity Simulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28181v1) - Simulates realistic long-horizon productivity work in synthetic computer environments for autonomous AI agents.
updates.reasoning: New paradigm for deploying AI agents that work autonomously in user environments, enabling on-demand app distribution.

updates.content: From Unstructured Recall to Schema-Grounded Memory: Reliable AI Memory via Iterative, Schema-Aware Extraction (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27906v1) - reliable persistent AI memory for application contexts.
updates.reasoning: New distribution mechanisms for AI-powered applications via schema-grounded memory.

updates.content: Outer-Crust Equations of State for Neutron Stars (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26952v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25914v1) - new agent evaluation mechanisms.
updates.reasoning: Advancements in agentic application deployment.

updates.content: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05170) - LLM agent autonomously designs and builds a hardware inference accelerator, demonstrating complete agent-as-distribution for complex hardware. Also: OpenSearch-VL: An Open Recipe for Frontier Multimodal Search Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05185) - open reproducible recipe for distributing multimodal search agent capabilities. Also: AlphaCrafter: A Full-Stack Multi-Agent Framework for Cross-Sectional Quantitative Trading (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05580) - agent-driven quantitative trading as a distributed service.
updates.reasoning: New distribution mechanisms multiply: agent-designed hardware accelerators replace traditional VLSI distribution, open agent recipes enable capability sharing, and full-stack multi-agent trading frameworks deliver financial AI as a distributed service.

predictions.title: Collaboration will move beyond source-code versioning
predictions.description: Git evolving to version agent interactions and records of intent.

updates.content: Evolving Idea Graphs with Learnable Edits-and-Commits for Multi-Agent Scientific Ideation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04922) - multi-agent system using edits-and-commits (version control for ideas rather than code) to evolve research ideas through graph-structured collaboration with persistent revision history. Also: Strat-Reasoner: Reinforcing Strategic Reasoning of LLMs in Multi-Agent Games (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04906) - reinforces strategic reasoning in multi-agent game-theoretic settings. Also: ARMATA: Auto-Regressive Multi-Agent Task Assignment (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04225) - auto-regressive task allocation for multi-agent coordination over spatially distributed areas.
updates.reasoning: Direct evidence: Evolving Idea Graphs explicitly uses Git-like edits-and-commits for multi-agent ideation, treating idea evolution as version-controlled collaboration. Strat-Reasoner and ARMATA extend multi-agent collaboration beyond code to strategic games and spatial task coordination.

updates.content: ARIS: Autonomous Research via Adversarial Multi-Agent Collaboration (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03042) - open-source research harness enabling autonomous scientific discovery through adversarial multi-agent collaboration. Also: PrismAgent: Illuminating Harm in Memes via a Zero-Shot Interpretable Multi-Agent Framework (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02940) - multi-agent collaboration for content moderation without training.
updates.reasoning: New collaboration paradigms for autonomous research through adversarial multi-agent teams and interpretable multi-agent frameworks for content moderation, extending collaboration beyond code to knowledge creation and social reasoning.

Also: DySRec: Dynamic Context-Aware Multi-Agent Collaboration (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00574).
updates.reasoning: New collaboration paradigms beyond code: sparsified information diffusion for multi-agent perception teams and context-aware psychological collaboration — moving toward interaction-level versioning.

Also: AgentFloor: How Far Up the tool use Ladder Can Small Open-Weight Models Go? (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00334) - agent workflow routing.

Also: The Inverse-Wisdom Law: Architectural Tribalism and the Consensus Paradox in Agentic Swarms (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27274v1) - challenges assumptions about multi-agent collaboration.

Also: Strat-Reasoner: Reinforcing Strategic Reasoning of LLMs in Multi-Agent Games (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04906) - multi-agent strategic reasoning in game-theoretic settings. predictions.title: AI-generated slop will raise the bar for quality
predictions.description: Differentiation shifts to taste, judgment, and intent as AI content floods.

updates.content: Intermediate Representations are Strong AI-Generated Image Detectors (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04358) - training-free AI-generated image detection using intermediate representations of pretrained models. Also: Detecting Deepfakes via Hamiltonian Dynamics (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04405) - physics-based deepfake detection using Hamiltonian dynamics to break the cycle of adversarial detector recalibration. Also: Curated AI beats frontier LLMs at pharma asset discovery (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04908) - demonstrates curated specialized AI platforms outperform general frontier LLMs for domain-specific knowledge tasks.
updates.reasoning: Training-free AI image detection and physics-based deepfake detection both raise the bar against AI-generated slop. The curated AI paper adds a new dimension: specialized curated data beats general frontier models, validating that differentiation shifts toward quality and domain expertise.

updates.content: GPT-Image-2 in the Wild: A Twitter Dataset of Self-Reported AI-Generated Images from the First Week of Deployment (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25370) - first published dataset analyzing real-world AI-generated images from GPT-Image-2 deployment. Also: IncreFA: Breaking the Static Wall of Generative Model Attribution (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.17736) - dynamic attribution framework that keeps pace with rapidly evolving generative models.
updates.reasoning: AI slop detection advancing into the real world with the first GPT-Image-2 deployment dataset and dynamic attribution that adapts to model evolution, both raising the bar for authenticated content.

updates.content: Automated In-the-Wild Data Collection for Continual AI Generated Image Detection (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02567) - continual learning for AI-gen image detection. Also: MelShield: Robust Mel-Domain Audio Watermarking for AI Generated Speech (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01515). Also: Fine-Tuning Pre-Trained Code Models for AI-Generated Code Detection (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01596).
updates.reasoning: AI slop detection maturing across modalities: continual image detection, audio watermarking for speech provenance, and code generation detection — raising the bar for authenticated content.

updates.content: TimeMark: A Trustworthy Time Watermarking Framework for Exact Generation-Time Recovery from AIGC (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.12216) - time-provenance watermarking for LLM text. Also: Who Gets Flagged? The Pluralistic Evaluation Gap in AI Content Watermarking (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.13776) - statistical disparities in watermark detection. Also: UniMark: Unified Adaptive Multi-bit Watermarking for Autoregressive Image Generators (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.11843).
updates.reasoning: Watermarking maturing rapidly — time-provenance, pluralistic evaluation, and multi-bit image watermarks raise the bar against AI slop.

updates.content: DYMAPIA: A Multi-Domain Framework for Detecting AI-based Video Manipulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.24426v1) - Multi-domain deepfake detection framework. Also: Dual-Guard: Dual-Channel Latent Watermarking for Provenance and Tamper Localization (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.19090v1) - watermarking for AI-generated content authentication.
updates.reasoning: Detection and authentication mechanisms evolving to counter AI-generated slop, raising the bar for verified content quality.

updates.content: AEGIS: A Holistic Benchmark for Evaluating Forensic Analysis of AI-Generated Academic Images (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28177v1) - Comprehensive benchmark for forensically detecting AI-generated images in academic contexts.
updates.reasoning: Quality differentiation mechanism for detecting AI-generated slop in academic publishing, raising the bar for authenticated content.

updates.content: DeepSignature: Digitally Signed, Content-Encoding Watermarks for Robust Image Authentication (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23016v1) - AI-generated content watermarking and authentication against synthetic slop.
updates.reasoning: Quality authentication mechanisms raised by the need to differentiate AI-generated content.

updates.content: Outer-Crust Equations of State for Neutron Stars (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26952v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25914v1) - focus on data viz benchmark quality.
updates.reasoning: New research focusing on agent evaluation quality.

updates.content: Chainwash: Multi-Step Rewriting Attacks on Diffusion Language Model Watermarks (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05503) - exposes new vulnerability class in diffusion LM watermarks, showing the adversarial arms race in AI-generated content authentication. Also: Safety-Critical Camera Reliability Monitoring for ADAS via Degradation-Aware Uncertainty Pattern Analysis (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05439) - reliability monitoring for safety-critical AI perception, raising the bar for production AI quality.
updates.reasoning: The watermark arms race continues: Chainwash exposes that diffusion LM watermarks can be rewritten away, driving the need for more robust authentication. Safety-critical reliability monitoring for ADAS cameras raises the bar for production AI system quality assessment.

predictions.title: Intelligence will move from digital to physical
predictions.description: Intent automation in the physical world; designing/automating physical systems.

updates.content: ConsisVLA-4D: Advancing Spatiotemporal Consistency in Efficient 3D-Perception and 4D-Reasoning for Robotic Manipulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05126) - 3D/4D perception-reasoning for robotic manipulation bridging digital VLA intelligence to physical interaction. Also: From Pixels to Tokens: A Systematic Study of Latent Action Supervision for VLA Models (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04678) - enables consistent VLA modeling across robot datasets. Also: From Reach to Insert: Tactile-Augmented Precision Assembly under Sub-Millimeter Tolerances (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04649) - tactile-augmented precision combining imitation learning with RL for sub-millimeter insertions. Also: Position: Embodied AI Requires a Privacy-Utility Trade-off (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05017) - addresses privacy implications of embodied AI moving into real homes.
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving to physical through: 3D/4D reasoning for robotic VLA manipulation, tactile-augmented precision assembly at sub-millimeter tolerances, latent action supervision enabling consistent robot learning, and privacy-aware embodied AI deployment.

updates.content: GS-Playground: A High-Throughput Photorealistic Simulator for Vision-Informed Robot Learning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25459) - massively parallel photorealistic simulator enabling vision-informed robot policy learning at scale. Also: PhysCodeBench: Benchmarking Physics-Aware Symbolic Simulation of 3D Scenes via Self-Corrective Multi-Agent Refinement (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.23580) - benchmarks physics-aware symbolic simulation translating natural language into executable 3D simulations.
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving to physical through high-throughput photorealistic robot simulators and physics-aware symbolic simulation that bridges natural language to executable physical environments.

updates.content: Embody4D: A Generalist 4D World Model for Embodied AI (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01799) - 4D world model bridging digital to physical. Also: MolmoAct2: Action Reasoning Models for Real-world Deployment (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02881) - real-world VLA robot deployment. Also: VILAS: Low-cost VLA Platform (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02037).
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving to physical: 4D world model for embodied AI, real-world robot deployment with MolmoAct2, and low-cost accessible robotics hardware democratizing physical intelligence.

updates.content: Embodied Interpretability: Linking Causal Understanding to Generalization in VLA Models (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00321) - causal understanding in robotic VLA policies. Also: Robot Learning from Human Videos: A Survey (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27621) - comprehensive survey of robot learning from video data. Also: KinDER: A Physical Reasoning Benchmark (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25788).
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving physical: causal VLA understanding, robot learning from video, and physical reasoning benchmarks.

updates.content: Rule-VLN: Bridging Perception and Compliance via Semantic Reasoning and Geometric Rectification (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.16993v1) - Embodied AI navigation agents evolving from reachability to social compliance. Also: PhyMix: Physically Consistent 3D Indoor Scene Generation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.10125v1) - physically grounded 3D generation for robotics and embodied AI.
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving to the physical world through embodied navigation with social compliance and physically consistent 3D scene understanding.

updates.content: OmniRobotHome: A Multi-Camera Platform for Real-Time Multiadic Human-Robot Interaction (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28197v1) - Real-time multi-human multi-robot collaboration in homes. Also: FlexiTac: Low-Cost Tactile Sensing for Robotic Systems (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28156v1).
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving from digital to physical with real-time collaborative robotics and accessible tactile sensing hardware.

updates.content: 3D Generation for Embodied AI and Robotic Simulation: A Survey (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26509v1) - comprehensive survey of 3D content generation for physical-world embodied AI.
updates.reasoning: Bridging digital intelligence to physical-world simulation and robotics.

updates.content: Anomalous Transport and Explicit Symmetry Breaking in Holography (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26949v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: Recursive Multi-Agent Systems (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25917v1) - relevance to system-level physical automation.
updates.reasoning: System architecture research.

updates.content: VLA-GSE: Boosting PEFT in VLA with Generalized and Specialized Experts (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06175) - parameter-efficient fine-tuning enabling more practical VLA deployment on physical robots. Also: DexSim2Real: Foundation Model-Guided Sim-to-Real Transfer for Generalizable Dexterous Manipulation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05241) - bridges simulation to real dexterous manipulation. Also: Creative Robot Tool Use by Counterfactual Reasoning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05411) - robot tool innovation through counterfactuals. Also: DexSynRefine: Synthesizing and Refining Human-Object Interaction Motion for Physically Feasible Dexterous Robot Actions (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05925) - physio-motion synthesis for dexterous manipulation.
updates.reasoning: Intelligence moving to physical through: efficient VLA fine-tuning for practical robot deployment, foundation model transfer enabling dexterous real-world manipulation, counterfactual reasoning for creative physical tool use, and physically feasible motion synthesis for dexterous robot actions.

predictions.title: Certifications for AI agents
predictions.description: Human-level certifications for agents replacing knowledge workers.

updates.content: Agent Island: A Saturation- and Contamination-Resistant Benchmark from Multiagent Games (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04312) - multiplayer simulation benchmark resistant to saturation and contamination for evaluating multi-agent LLM capabilities. Also: AgentTrust: Runtime Safety Evaluation and Interception for AI Agent Tool Use (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04785) - runtime safety system intercepting unsafe agent tool calls. Also: DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap): A Controllable and Interactive Red-Teaming Platform for AI Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04808) - interactive red-teaming platform for AI agent security evaluation. Also: AuditRepairBench: A Paired-Execution Trace Corpus for Evaluator-Channel Ranking Instability in Agent Repair (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04624) - exposes leaderboard instability in agent repair evaluation. Also: Deployment-Relevant Alignment Cannot Be Inferred from Model-Level Evaluation Alone (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04454) - argues agent certification requires deployment-level evaluation.
updates.reasoning: Massive expansion of agent certification infrastructure: contamination-resistant benchmarks, runtime safety evaluation and interception, interactive red-teaming platforms, audit tools for benchmark integrity, and a critical position paper arguing model-level evaluation is insufficient for deployment certification.

updates.content: From Intent to Execution: Composing Agentic Workflows with Agent Recommendation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03986) - automated agent workflow composition and recommendation, certifying agent selection for user intents. Also: Workspace-Bench 1.0: Benchmarking AI Agents on Workspace Tasks with Large-Scale File Dependencies (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.03596) - benchmarks agents on complex workspace tasks with heterogeneous file dependencies. Also: AcademiClaw: When Students Set Challenges for AI Agents (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02661) - academic-level benchmark authored by university students for evaluating sophisticated agent capabilities.
updates.reasoning: Agent certification infrastructure expanding: automated agent recommendation for workflow certification, workspace task benchmarks with complex file dependencies, and academic-level benchmarks challenging agents with real student-designed tasks.

updates.content: DataClaw: A Process-Oriented Agent Benchmark for Exploratory Real-World Data Analysis (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.02503) - benchmark for autonomous data analysis agents. Also: 12 Angry AI Agents: Evaluating Multi-Agent LLM Decision-Making (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.01986) - novel multi-agent evaluation framework. Also: Claw-Eval-Live: Live Agent Benchmark (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28139v2).
updates.reasoning: Agent certification infrastructure expanding: process-oriented data analysis benchmarks, cinematic jury-style multi-agent evaluation, and live-updating benchmarks that track evolving workflows.

updates.content: AgentReputation: A Decentralized Agentic AI Reputation Framework (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.00073) - decentralized reputation for agent marketplaces. Also: Agent Name Service (ANS): Trust Layer for Secure AI Agent Discovery, Identity, and Governance in Kubernetes (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26997). Also: SciHorizon-DataEVA: Agentic AI-Readiness Evaluation (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26645).
updates.reasoning: Certification infrastructure advancing: decentralized reputation, Kubernetes identity/governance, and AI-readiness evaluation.

updates.content: Claw-Eval-Live: A Live Agent Benchmark for Evolving Real-World Workflows (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28139v1) - Live-updating benchmark for evaluating agent capabilities in real-world workflows. Also: What Makes a Good Terminal-Agent Benchmark Task (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28093v1) - guidelines for agent evaluation design.
updates.reasoning: Benchmark evolution toward certification-style evaluation of agent capabilities in dynamic real-world settings.

updates.content: AEGIS: A Holistic Benchmark for Evaluating Forensic Analysis of AI-Generated Academic Images (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.28177v1) - Benchmark for certifying AI content. Also: ANCORA: Learning to Question via Manifold-Anchored Self-Play for Verifiable Reasoning (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27644v1).
updates.reasoning: Benchmark-based certification frameworks and verifiable reasoning infrastructure for agent quality assurance.

updates.content: TRUST: A Framework for Decentralized AI Service v.0.1 (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.27132v1) - decentralized verification framework for LLMs and multi-agent systems in high-stakes domains.
updates.reasoning: Direct work on certification/verification frameworks for AI agent systems.

updates.content: Turning the TIDE: Cross-Architecture Distillation for Diffusion Large Language Models (http://arxiv.org/abs/2604.26951v1)
updates.reasoning: Daily arXiv update for this prediction category.

updates.content: DV-World: Benchmarking Data Visualization Agents (https://arxiv.org/abs/2604.25914v1) - benchmark-based agent performance evaluation.
updates.reasoning: Benchmarking and performance standard research.

updates.content: Safety Certification is Classification (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06087) - reframes safety certification of dynamical systems as classification, directly relevant to agent certification. Also: Partial Evidence Bench: Benchmarking Authorization-Limited Evidence in Agentic Systems (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05379) - benchmarks agents under authorization constraints. Also: Authorization Propagation in Multi-Agent AI Systems: Identity Governance as Infrastructure (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05440) - identity and authorization governance for multi-agent systems. Also: Deployment-Relevant Alignment Cannot Be Inferred from Model-Level Evaluation Alone (http://arxiv.org/abs/2605.04454) - argues agent certification requires deployment-level evaluation.
updates.reasoning: Agent certification infrastructure expands rapidly: classification-based safety certification, authorization-limited benchmark evaluation, identity governance as foundational infrastructure for multi-agent systems, and a critical argument that deployment-level evaluation is necessary for agent certification.
